title: Network Segmentation — Cross-VLAN RDP Allowed by Mis-Tuned ACL

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001 A.8.22 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO 27001 A.8.22 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.6.43: Network Segmentation — Cross-VLAN RDP Allowed by Mis-Tuned ACL.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.6.43' running on pan:traffic, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=network sourcetype=pan:traffic earliest=-24h app=rdp action=allow
| where src_zone!=dest_zone AND match(dest_zone,"(?i)pci|prod_db")
| stats count by src, dest, rule
description: Flags RDP sessions crossing VLAN boundaries that should be blocked by segmentation policy.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes network and sourcetypes pan:traffic. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for Network Segmentation — Cross-VLAN RDP Allowed by Mis-Tuned ACL. ISO 27001 tells us what good security management looks like, and we document that we follow it.
implementation: (1) Validate zone tags; (2) emergency ACL review; (3) document compensating jump hosts. Indexes required: `index=network`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:traffic`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Palo Alto Networks TA.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:traffic`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Validate zone tags; (2) emergency ACL review; (3) document compensating jump hosts.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:traffic earliest=-24h app=rdp action=allow
| where src_zone!=dest_zone AND match(dest_zone,"(?i)pci|prod_db")
| stats count by src, dest, rule
```

Understanding this SPL

**Network Segmentation — Cross-VLAN RDP Allowed by Mis-Tuned ACL (A.8.22)** — Flags RDP sessions crossing VLAN boundaries that should be blocked by segmentation policy.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto Networks TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype=pan:traffic, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where src_zone!=dest_zone AND match(dest_zone,"(?i)pci|prod_db")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src, dest, rule** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Heatmap
knownFalsePositives: Incidents may bunch during major events, vulnerability bulletin response, or controlled exercises.
